segments.text: After this, the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision:
Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, your very great reward.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Abram said,
Sovereign Lord, what can you give me since I remain childless and the one who will inherit my estate is Eliezer of Damascus?
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: And Abram said,
You have given me no children; so a servant in my household will be my heir.
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Then the word of the Lord came to him:
This man will not be your heir, but a son who is your own flesh and blood will be your heir.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He took him outside and said,
Look up at the sky and count the stars—if indeed you can count them.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then he said to him,
So shall your offspring be.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Abram believed the Lord, and he credited it to him as righteousness.
He also said to him,
I am the Lord, who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to take possession of it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Abram said,
Sovereign Lord, how can I know that I will gain possession of it?
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: So the Lord said to him,
Bring me a heifer, a goat and a ram, each three years old, along with a dove and a young pigeon.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Abram brought all these to him, cut them in two and arranged the halves opposite each other; the birds, however, he did not cut in half.
Then birds of prey came down on the carcasses, but Abram drove them away.
As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and a thick and dreadful darkness came over him.
Then the Lord said to him,
Know for certain that for four hundred years your descendants will be strangers in a country not their own and that they will be enslaved and mistreated there.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But I will punish the nation they serve as slaves, and afterward they will come out with great possessions.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You, however, will go to your ancestors in peace and be buried at a good old age.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In the fourth generation your descendants will come back here, for the sin of the Amorites has not yet reached its full measure.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When the sun had set and darkness had fallen, a smoking firepot with a blazing torch appeared and passed between the pieces.
On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram and said,
To your descendants I give this land, from the Wadi of Egypt to the great river, the Euphrates—
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the land of the Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites,
Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,
Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites and Jebusites.
